page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-4GB is a virtual server instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It runs on the KVM hypervisor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor features 16 MB of L3 cache alongside hardware support for AVX, AES, and SHA_NI cryptographic extensions. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance characteristics. The shared AMD EPYC processor delivers poor multi-core performance, placing in the bottom 10% in stress-ng tests, while single-core Geekbench scores reach average levels. Memory bandwidth is limited, with uncached large-block read and write speeds falling into the bottom 10%. Cryptographic and compression tasks show weak to poor performance, though single-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves average marks. Static web serving throughput and database operations are also positioned in the lower tiers.

Given its shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage, this instance represents a budget-friendly option for resource-constrained environments. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for development, testing, and light utility workloads that do not require sustained high CPU or memory throughput. Appropriate workloads include basic text processing, minor background tasks, lightweight web serving, and small-scale application prototyping.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-4GB is an entry-level virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by a shared AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, the instance features 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark evaluations place its performance largely in the lower tiers, with poor multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth results, though it achieves average marks in single-core Geekbench and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for non-production environments, basic utility tasks, and lightweight applications. It is best suited for development sandboxes, minor text processing, and small-scale prototyping where sustained high performance is not required.
